strikeOverview.title: Strike Overview
strikeOverview.description: Key details about the ongoing Air Canada flight attendant strike

storyIntroduction.title: The Human Cost Behind the Numbers
storyIntroduction.subtitle: A decade of wage stagnation meets financial absurdity: Why Air Canada's strike losses dwarf the cost of fair compensation
storyIntroduction.nearlyDecadeDesc: Flight attendants faced stagnant wages, unpaid ground duty hours, and rising living costs while performing critical safety roles.
storyIntroduction.increaseOfferedDesc: over four years, plus ground pay adjustments and improved pensions.
storyIntroduction.unionRejectionDesc: Flight attendants rejected the offer, citing incomplete compensation for all hours worked and inflation concerns.
storyIntroduction.dailyLossDesc: what fair compensation would cost annually.
storyIntroduction.financialAbsurdityDesc: Every day this strike continues, Air Canada hemorrhages more money than it would cost to provide fair compensation to all 10,511 flight attendants for an entire year. This isn't just an economic disaster—it's an ethical failure where keeping planes grounded costs more than treating employees fairly.

storyHighlights.numbersStory: The numbers tell an shocking story
highlights: Every day of strike costs Air Canada $100 million.
That's the same as burning $9,500 per flight attendant per day.
After just 10 days, Air Canada will have lost the equivalent of $95,000 per flight attendant.
Meanwhile, the union is asking for fair pay across all hours worked — a fraction of what Air Canada is already losing.

unpaidHours.title: The Unpaid Hours Crisis
unpaidHours.subtitle: Understanding what flight attendants don't get paid for
unpaidHours.description: Flight attendants perform critical safety duties during 'ground time' that goes completely uncompensated, creating significant financial hardship despite full-time schedules.
unpaidDuties: Boarding assistance and passenger safety briefings (30-45 minutes per flight)
Pre-flight safety equipment checks and cabin preparation (15-30 minutes)
Post-flight cleaning, restocking, and security procedures (20-30 minutes)
Passenger deplaning assistance and gate coordination (15-20 minutes)
Mandatory crew briefings and schedule coordination (varies)
Aircraft turnaround duties between connecting flights (30+ minutes)
unpaidHours.impactDescription: These unpaid hours add up to 2-4 hours per shift. For a full-time flight attendant working 80 hours per month, this represents 20-30% of their actual working time going uncompensated.
unpaidHours.safetyDescription: Many of these unpaid duties are safety-critical. When essential safety work isn't valued through compensation, it creates systemic issues that can affect aviation safety standards and employee wellbeing.
unpaidHours.financialImpact: A flight attendant earning $50,000 annually could be losing $10,000-15,000 per year in unpaid work—equivalent to working for free 2-3 months annually.

strikeInfo.title: Air Canada Flight Attendant Strike Information
strikeInfo.description: Comprehensive overview of the ongoing labor dispute between Air Canada and the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE), representing flight attendants.
strikeInfo.strikeDescription: CUPE-represented flight attendants commence work stoppage after eight months of unsuccessful negotiations, creating significant operational disruptions across Canada's aviation network.
strikeInfo.companyOfferDetails: 38% total compensation increase over four years
strikeInfo.primaryIssueDetails: Ground pay for all required duties and working hours
strikeInfo.unionPositionDetails: Full compensation for all working time, not just flight time
strikeInfo.customerRebooking: Customer rebooking and refund programs active

sources.title: Data Sources & Verification
sources.description: All figures and information are sourced from official statements, verified news reports, and public records. Hover over any data point to view its source.
sources.lossEstimationDesc: $100M daily impact based on TD Cowen analyst estimate of C$300M ($217M) for a 3-day strike, extrapolated to daily figures.
sources.perAttendantImpactDesc: Calculated as total daily impact divided by 10,511 CUPE-represented flight attendants.
sources.strikeTimelineCalcDesc: Counter begins at officially announced strike commencement: August 16, 2025, 1:00 AM Eastern Time.
sources.realTimeUpdatesDesc: All calculations update continuously based on precise elapsed time since strike initiation.
sources.disclaimerText: This website is an independent informational resource about the ongoing Air Canada flight attendants strike. All financial figures and operational details are estimates based on public reports and may not reflect actual losses or outcomes. This site is not affiliated with Air Canada or any union and should not be construed as legal or financial advice.

footerSections.dataMethodologyDesc: Financial projections compiled from verified official sources and respected financial news outlets.
footerSections.analystProjections: Based on analyst projections from TD Cowen and industry reports. Real-time calculations provide illustrative impact modeling.

footer.disclaimer: Data sourced from public reports and official statements. Projections are estimates based on available information.
